fix: v2.0.1 bugfix — spec compliance, validation, and safety hardening

- Fix artifact slug validation to enforce lowercase+hyphens per spec (was allowing uppercase/underscores)
- Add peek_next_sequence() to prevent sequence counter consumption in read-only config generation
- Fix CLI status command to use safe blink listing (symlink rejection) instead of raw glob
- Add bidirectional origin blink validation (relational '^' ↔ Born from: Origin)
- Improve sentence counter to handle common abbreviations (Dr., e.g., etc.)
- Expand check_escalation() to scan all directories (was missing profile/archive)
- Fix docs: directory count, manifest description, stale test count, dead blink references
- Display implementation version alongside spec version in CLI status
- Fix test helpers using wrong relational sigil for origin blinks

All 561 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 31.3 — test chat auth, preflight gates, test mode labels

Root cause: window.__firebaseAuth was NEVER assigned anywhere in the
codebase. All getToken() calls that checked it skipped to localStorage,
which held an invalid custom token (not an ID token). Every authenticated
API call after signup was using the wrong token type.

Fix: Import `auth` from firebase.ts directly in DeveloperSandbox,
TestWorkerPanel, and PublishPreflight. All getToken/getFreshToken helpers
now use the real Firebase Auth instance. Also sync fresh tokens back to
localStorage for consistency.

P0 — Test chat 401:
- getToken() in TestWorkerPanel now uses firebaseAuth.currentUser
- signInWithCustomToken in handleInlineSignup uses firebaseAuth directly
  (removed dynamic import, uses static import)

P1 — Preflight gates:
- Gates 1 & 2 now work: Accept opens agreement page / accepts inline,
  falls back to local acceptance if backend unreachable
- Gate 3: Test mode shows "Simulated — Stripe Identity" with yellow TEST
  badge, mock 1.5s verification delay
- Gate 4: Now requires text bio input, calls Alex to generate 2-3
  sentence expertise summary, displays below for review
- Gates 5 & 6: Test mode shows yellow "Simulated" badges instead of
  green "Done". "simulated" string state vs true boolean distinguishes
  real vs test completions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Anthropic STT-only mode: transcription event never fired due to early response cancel

Cancelling on response.created prevented conversation.item.input_audio_transcription.completed
from firing — so Claude never received the caller's speech. Moved the cancel to
response.output_item.added, which fires after transcription is complete but before
OpenAI generates any GPT output tokens. This lets Whisper finish transcribing, then
kills the GPT response before it produces anything.

commit.message:
docs: update README for Phase 10 — SHA-3, PBKDF2, 5G KDF, key wrapping

What's New table:
- Add PBKDF2 (CKM_PKCS5_PBKD2) row — BIP39/SLIP-0010 seed derivation
- Add ECDH1-with-KDF row — X9.63 KDF for 5G SUCI deconcealment
- Add SHA-3 signature variants row (ECDSA-SHA3 both engines; RSA-SHA3 C++ only)

PKCS#11 Mechanisms:
- New section: Classical Signatures — SHA-3 variants (ECDSA + RSA, with CKM values)
- Key Derivation: add CKM_PKCS5_PBKD2 + CKM_ECDH1_DERIVE with KDF comment

Rust engine:
- Functions table: Key Management row adds C_WrapKeyAuthenticated + C_UnwrapKeyAuthenticated
- Export count updated: 63 → 65 (49 implemented, 8 multi-part stubs, 8 admin stubs)
- AES algorithms: add Authenticated Wrap/Unwrap
- Add note: RSA-SHA3 and ECDH1-with-KDF are C++ only

Roadmap:
- Add Phase 10: SHA-3 variants, PBKDF2, 5G KDF, key wrapping completeness (9 items)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: switch to NN heuristic, extract helpers, improve code quality

- Default control changed to list(method = "nn", rep = 20) for
  nearest-neighbour TSP at both zone and tract level — better for
  sliding-window fieldwork where consecutive proximity matters more
  than global tour length
- Extract .sequence_zones() and .sequence_tracts_with_orientation()
  from surveyzones_sequence() to keep functions under 50 lines
- Add .seriate_quietly() for scoped warning suppression (only muffles
  known-harmless TSP control and %dopar% warnings)
- Strip TSP-specific control keys for non-TSP methods (fixes OLO/Spectral)
- Use .by instead of group_by/ungroup, join_by() instead of by = "..."
- Use purrr::map_dbl() instead of vapply() for consistency
- Align .seriate_1d() and surveyzones_sequence_tracts() defaults
- Add tests: NN consecutive proximity, control = NULL override
- Seed NN-dependent tests with withr::local_seed(42)
- Rewrite orientation test to be zone-order-independent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add complete IronClick SaaS platform

Multi-tenant platform for managing and selling digital services (streaming, IPTV, software).

- PHP 8.2 + MySQL backend with MVC architecture
- Multi-tenant routing via subdomains (localhost support with ?panel= param)
- 5-role hierarchy: super_admin, panel, distribuidor, vendedor, cliente
- 12 database tables with soft delete, encryption (AES-256-GCM), bcrypt auth
- 13 controllers, 11 models, full API routes
- Dashboard with metrics, stock, charts (Chart.js)
- Modules: servicios, cuentas, perfiles, ventas, clientes, proveedores, creditos, IMAP, reportes, configuracion
- Public store (tienda) per panel with catalog, FAQ, order tracking
- WhatsApp integration with 12 configurable templates
- Landing page with plans (estandar/vip), registration with contact info
- CSRF protection, rate limiting, secure sessions
- PWA with service worker, glassmorphism dark UI
- Tailwind CSS + Vanilla JS frontend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: IronCick
repository.description: sistema de gestion de ventas

commit.message:
Milestone 5: warm background, desktop layouts, error polish, save toast

App.jsx (all milestones consolidated):
- LoadingScreen: text updated to "Gemini is analyzing…"
- ErrorBanner: friendly one-liner, raw error logged to console only
- Toast: green fixed-bottom "✓ Brew saved to history ☕" shown 2.5s after save
- AppHeader rendered on setup/recipe/rate/history; timer screen left dark/full-screen
- Setup screen: bg-amber-50, md:grid-cols-2 two-column layout (selectors | upload)
- Recipe screen: bg-amber-50, md:grid-cols-[3fr_2fr] with sticky grind+actions right panel
- Rate screen: max-w-xl wrapper
- History screen: AppHeader + BrewHistory
- V60 size buttons: combined "01 · 1 cup" label format
- beanPhoto passed to RecipeCard
- onRate prop passed to BrewTimer for post-brew shortcut

injury_api.py:

  (already in the ~8MB response, just never read before).

  Starters always appear first and are what the AI prioritises in
  its probability reasoning.

commit.message:
fix: use local timezone for date-based features (check-in, usage stats)

Root cause: `new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)` returns UTC date,
causing date-based features to be off by up to ±1 day for non-UTC users.

Check-in / assistant workspace:
- Add `getLocalDateString()` utility, replace all UTC date comparisons
- Schema v2→v3 migration: normalize lastCheckInDate only when it matches
  UTC today (ambiguous window); leave past dates untouched so check-in
  triggers correctly after upgrade
- Runtime compat in `needsDailyCheckIn`: accept UTC today as fallback
  for edge cases the migration cannot catch
- `needsDailyCheckIn(state, now?)` accepts optional time for testability

Usage stats:
- Replace single-offset SQL bucketing (`DATE(datetime(..., '+N minutes'))`)
  with JS-side per-row aggregation using `getLocalDateString(new Date(utc))`
- Date methods account for historical DST offset at each message's timestamp
- `getTokenUsageStats(days, now?)` accepts optional time to pin window start
- Window boundary via `localDayStartAsUTC()` is already DST-aware

Tests (new file: timezone-boundaries.test.ts, 22 tests):
- getLocalDateString at UTC+9/UTC-5/UTC boundaries with fixed timestamps
- needsDailyCheckIn: compat suppression, clearly-past trigger, local match
- v2→v3 migration: targeted normalization, past-date preservation, null safe
- localDayStartAsUTC: UTC+8/UTC-5 midnight boundaries
- Usage stats bucketing: UTC+9 cross-day, summary window, DST spring-forward
- All usage stats tests use pinned `now` — no time-bomb from fixed test data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(storyforge): add ResizeObserver to Narrative Drift canvas

The canvas was not filling the full image-wrap width because layout
reflows (sidebar rendering, flex settling) changed the container size
after initial canvas creation. ResizeObserver watches the container
and resizes the canvas when dimensions change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add M0052 X-COP inversion + lensing cross-check prompt with new cluster data

M0052 combines the X-COP response function inversion (from M0050/M0051)
with three new lensing-calibrated datasets: CLASH NFW (20 clusters,
Umetsu+2016), SMACS J0723 (Liu+2023), and Frontier Fields kappa maps.
Key finding: CLASH lensing deficit at R500 = 1.80x, larger than
hydrostatic ~1.6x, confirming the deficit is not a hydrostatic artifact.

Also includes M0049 closing the xi-partition thread (Verdict 3 confirmed).

Fix Python alert service: retry/CB semantics, state-change logging, add tests

  retry sequence counts as one failure toward fail_max (was: tenacity decorator
  wrapping _make_request inside the CB, causing up to 15 calls before opening)

  OPEN → HALF-OPEN) so state changes are observable in service logs
